description.p: Calix is a native calendar for Linux, built with GTK4 and libadwaita. It syncs both ways with Google Calendar, iCloud, and any CalDAV server (Fastmail, Nextcloud, Radicale, and more), and keeps everything in a local SQLite store so the app stays fast and works offline.

ul.li: Swipeable month, week, and day views
Two-way sync with Google, iCloud, and generic CalDAV accounts
Drag events to move or resize them, with a live snapped preview
Create events by clicking or right-clicking anywhere on the grid
Show or hide individual calendars from a native sidebar
Passwords and tokens stored in the system keyring, never in files

description.p: Calix now syncs automatically in the background — on launch and every 15 minutes — so connected calendars stay current without a manual refresh. An expired Google sign-in surfaces a clear reconnect prompt instead of failing quietly, and this release fixes several issues across sync, storage, and iCalendar handling.

Week and day grids gain a zoom control and a live current-time marker, event titles show in full as a hover tooltip, and the window now matches the active Omarchy theme's colors. This release also scopes CalDAV account identity to the (username, server) pair and fixes seven audit findings across sync, storage, and drag handling.
Generic CalDAV account support with two-way sync, drag to move and resize events in the week and day grids, and right-click event creation.
